source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/09_Usability/260303 사용목적 수정(CE 반영)/A-UFER-HNX Usability Formative Evaluation Report (Rev.1).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Formative usability evaluation report for the Portable X-ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents formative assessment activities intended to filter out user interface design flaws that could potentially result in hazardous use errors.
keyPoints: The variant model HnX-PB changed only exterior design compared to HnX-P1; interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software are stated to be the same.
No additional usability validation will be conducted for addition of the variant model HnX-PB.
Formative evaluation was conducted through usability survey questions based on a given scenario with 5 qualified participants.
Questionnaire scores must be 3 points or higher, and comprehensive judgment is limited to cases that passed usability test evaluation.
All test results were judged appropriate, and the product usability was confirmed as safe and effective.

concepts.name: Formative usability evaluation
concepts.evidence: This report is intended to perform a formative assessment based on various factors including the complexity of the medical device, the potential for hazardous use errors, development schedule, and budget

concepts.name: Hazardous use errors
concepts.evidence: user interface design flaws that could potentially result in hazardous use errors

concepts.name: Variant model usability validation rationale
concepts.evidence: Therefore, no additional usability validation will be conducted for the addition of the variant model(HnX-PB).

concepts.name: Participant qualification
concepts.evidence: The participants must be qualified for the user profile, and the survey is conducted with 5 participants.

concepts.name: Usability questionnaire acceptance criterion
concepts.evidence: When the results of the questionnaire are converted into a score, the score must be 3 points or higher.

concepts.name: Usability re-evaluation triggers
concepts.evidence: The usability evaluation must be re-evaluated in the following cases: Changes in the design of the product; When there are customer requirements related to the usability of the product

evidence.claim: HnX-PB changed only exterior design compared to HnX-P1, while interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software are the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model(HnX-PB) has only changed the exterior design compared to the existing model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: No additional usability validation will be conducted for addition of HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, no additional usability validation will be conducted for the addition of the variant model(HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: Formative evaluation was conducted through a survey on product usability.
evidence.evidenceText: Formative evaluation of the product is conducted through a survey on product usability.

evidence.claim: Participants are provided with a product user manual and training before the survey.
evidence.evidenceText: Before the survey, the participants are provided with a product user manual and training.

evidence.claim: The survey is conducted with 5 participants who must be qualified for the user profile.
evidence.evidenceText: The participants must be qualified for the user profile, and the survey is conducted with 5 participants.

evidence.claim: User profile criteria include doctor or radiologist, medical or radiology license, and relevant bachelor's degree or higher.
evidence.evidenceText:
Occupation: Doctor or Radiologist 직업: 의사 또는 방사선사

Skills and Qualifications: Medical Licensee or Radiology Licensee

Knowledge and Education: Bachelor's degree or higher from a medical school or Bachelor's degree or higher in a radiology-related field

evidence.claim: Questionnaire score acceptance criterion is 3 points or higher.
evidence.evidenceText: Evaluation of the results of the questionnaire: When the results of the questionnaire are converted into a score, the score must be 3 points or higher.

evidence.claim: Usability evaluation must be re-evaluated after product design changes or usability-related customer requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability evaluation must be re-evaluated in the following cases: Changes in the design of the product; When there are customer requirements related to the usability of the product

evidence.claim: All five listed participants were radiologists and received final pass decisions with survey result 4.7.
evidence.evidenceText: Occupation: Radiologist ... Survey Result ... Pass ... 4.7 ... Final decision ... Pass

evidence.claim: All test results were judged appropriate and product usability was confirmed safe and effective.
evidence.evidenceText: All test results were judged to be appropriate, and it was confirmed that the usability of this product was safe and effective.

evidence.claim: Key findings included ergonomic design, X-RAY emission caution, button and status LED identification, and Bluetooth image preview through LCD.
evidence.evidenceText:
Key Findings:

Ergonomic design that is easy to hold and use by hand is required.

Caution is required regarding X-RAY emission.

Each button and status LED should be easy to identify.

A function that can preview images captured via Bluetooth function through LCD is required.

evidence.claim: Implemented improvements included ergonomic hand holding design, X-RAY caution symbol and warning message, clearer button and color-coded status LED identification, and Bluetooth captured-image preview.
evidence.evidenceText:
Improvements Implemented:

Ergonomic design for easy hand holding

Addition of X-RAY caution symbol and warning message

Clarification of identification of each button and color-coded status LED for easy status identification

Captured images can be previewed via Bluetooth function
